section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports decreased sensation to light touch.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Decreased sensation to light touch is typically a physical exam finding; however, phrased as a patient report it can reasonably appear in HPI without substantially misleading the reader.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports numbness to the foot and reports tingling.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a subjective symptom description rather than an observed exam finding; its placement in Findings is slightly disorganized but not seriously misleading.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports sharp, stabbing back pain.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Sharp, stabbing back pain is a subjective symptom and belongs in the HPI; listing it under Findings is a mild organizational issue.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient has previously tried physical therapy.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is past treatment history, not a new intervention or order; it fits better in HPI as prior management rather than in the current plan.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient has previously been offered surgery.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Being previously offered surgery is part of prior treatment history, not an active plan item; it is more appropriate in HPI.
